mainEntity.name: Can a dormant LLP be struck off?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, under Section 75 of LLP Act. LLPs that have not filed for 2 consecutive years or not carried on business for 2 years can apply for strike off.

mainEntity.name: How to close/wind up an LLP?

acceptedAnswer.text: File Form 24 (Application for strike-off) with RoC. Requires consent of all partners. All pending returns and compliances must be cleared. No assets, liabilities, or pending proceedings. Alternatively, voluntary winding up under IBC Section 59.

mainEntity.name: What is the cost of LLP closure?

acceptedAnswer.text: Government fee for Form 24: ₹50 (partner contribution up to ₹1 lakh). Professional fee: ₹5,000-₹10,000. Must clear all pending annual filings and pay any penalties before filing closure application.

mainEntity.name: Can an LLP be struck off if annual returns are not filed?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. RoC can initiate strike-off of LLPs that have not filed annual returns (Form 8 and Form 11) for 2 or more consecutive years. Partners remain liable for dues and penalties even after strike-off.

mainEntity.name: What are the compliance requirements before LLP closure?

acceptedAnswer.text: File all pending Form 8 (Statement of Account) and Form 11 (Annual Return). Clear all tax liabilities (GST, IT). Cancel GST registration. Close bank accounts. Settle all debts and distribute surplus.

mainEntity.name: Is NOC from Income Tax needed for LLP closure?

acceptedAnswer.text: Not mandatory for strike-off route. But all pending ITRs must be filed and taxes paid. For voluntary winding up under IBC, Income Tax clearance is part of the process.
